steps.title: Identify the uncertainty the discovery revealed
steps.content: The passage concludes: “This means that Bellamy may not have created the pledge after all.” The discovery of a pre-1892 article directly undermined the established belief about who authored the pledge.

steps.title: Match uncertainty to the answer
steps.content: B identifies what became uncertain: the original author's identity. The pre-1892 evidence challenges Bellamy's authorship claim, so we no longer know who truly wrote it. The other choices reference facts the passage never mentions. That matches Choice B — The identity of the person who first wrote the pledge.
